arguments.task: Open and read official Datadog pages that support these fields: API description, authentication methods, rate limiting, pricing/access tier, and whether Datadog has an official MCP server/integration. Prefer canonical first-party pages such as docs.datadoghq.com and datadoghq.com. Navigate to the Datadog API docs, authentication docs, rate limit docs, pricing/plans page if needed, and any official MCP page if it exists. Return concise extracted findings with the exact page URLs actually read for each finding. Do not rely on search snippets.

input.thought: Start a browser task to inspect official Datadog docs and pricing pages directly.

raw_result.description: Observability platform with an HTTP REST API for programmatic access to Datadog resources and data.
raw_result.auth_notes: Datadog documents API keys and application keys. Requests use the DD-API-KEY header, and some endpoints also require DD-APPLICATION-KEY. The application key is a Datadog-specific developer credential, mapped here as other rather than OAuth or Basic.
raw_result.gating_reason: Datadog publicly offers a Free tier on its pricing page, and API docs are publicly available without partner or sales gating.

api_surface.breadth: Public API reference covers multiple Datadog product areas and endpoints, with resource-oriented URLs, JSON responses, and standard HTTP response codes.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Datadog has an official MCP Server product/docs page describing connectivity between AI agents/IDEs and Datadog observability data.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Datadog documents endpoint-specific rate limits and standard headers including X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset, and X-RateLimit-Period; exceeding limits returns HTTP 429.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs explicitly describe the API as HTTP REST with JSON responses, publish auth and rate-limit guidance, provide a public pricing page with a Free tier, and publish an official MCP Server page.
